f.description: Fixed cost to open warehouse j

c.description: Transportation cost if all of customer i's demand is served by warehouse j (already accounts for d_i)

assumptions.description: Demand of each customer is positive.

code.python: assert all(d[i] > 0 for i in range(n))

assumptions.description: Capacity of each warehouse is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(u[j] >= 0 for j in range(m))

assumptions.description: Transportation cost for each customer-warehouse pair is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(c[i][j] >= 0 for i in range(n) for j in range(m))

assumptions.description: Fixed cost to open each warehouse is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(f[j] >= 0 for j in range(m))

T.description: Set of large warehouses: those with capacity at least the maximum customer demand.
T.formulation: T = \{ j \in J : u_j \geq \max_{i \in I} d_i \}

code.python:
max_d = max(d)
T = [j for j in range(m) if u[j] >= max_d]

u_max_small.description: Maximum capacity among non-large (small) warehouses; 0 if all warehouses are large.
u_max_small.formulation: u^{\text{small}}_{\max} = \max_{j \in J \setminus T} u_j \;\text{(} 0 \text{ if } J \setminus T = \emptyset \text{)}

code.python: u_max_small = max((u[j] for j in range(m) if j not in T), default=0)

H.description: Set of hard customers: those whose demand exceeds the largest small-warehouse capacity, so they can only be served by a large warehouse.

code.python: H = [i for i in range(n) if d[i] > u_max_small]
D_H = sum(d[i] for i in H)

k_T.description: Minimum number of large warehouses whose capacities (taken in non-increasing order) sum to at least D_H. Equals 0 if H is empty.
k_T.formulation: k_T = \min \Big\{ k \geq 0 : \sum_{\ell=1}^{k} u_{(\ell)} \geq D_H \Big\},\quad u_{(1)} \geq u_{(2)} \geq \cdots \text{ enumerating } \{u_j : j \in T\}

x.description: 1 if customer i is assigned to warehouse j, 0 otherwise

y.description: 1 if warehouse j is opened, 0 otherwise

constraints.description: Each customer is assigned to exactly one warehouse.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(gp.quicksum(x[i, j] for j in range(m)) == 1 for i in range(n))

constraints.description: Capacity: total demand assigned to each warehouse cannot exceed its capacity times whether it is open.
constraints.formulation: \sum_{i \in I} d_i \, x_{ij} \leq u_j \, y_j \quad \forall j \in J

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(gp.quicksum(d[i] * x[i, j] for i in range(n)) <= u[j] * y[j] for j in range(m))

constraints.description: T-Cover Bound (EC3): at least k_T warehouses must be opened.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstr(gp.quicksum(y[j] for j in range(m)) >= k_T)

objective.description: Minimize the total fixed opening cost plus transportation cost.
objective.formulation: \min \sum_{j \in J} f_j \, y_j + \sum_{i \in I} \sum_{j \in J} c_{ij} \, x_{ij}

code.gurobipy:
model.setObjective(
    gp.quicksum(f[j] * y[j] for j in range(m)) +
    gp.quicksum(c[i][j] * x[i, j] for i in range(n) for j in range(m)),
    GRB.MINIMIZE
)

notes: Small warehouses are those with capacity less than the maximum customer demand. Hard customers are those whose demand exceeds the largest small-warehouse capacity, so they can only be served by large warehouses. To cover the hard customers, we need to open at least $k_T$ large warehouses, where $k_T$ is the minimum number of large warehouses whose capacities sum to at least the total demand of hard customers.
